title: Sense and Sensibility
author: Austen, Jane
summary: After the death of a kindly uncle, the Dashwood family faces sudden insecurity: Norland estate passes by entail to a nephew’s son, leaving Mr. Henry Dashwood little power to provide for his widow and three daughters. When Henry soon dies, his widow and daughters are left with scant funds. His son John, heir to Norland, promises assistance but, under his wife Fanny’s influence, contemplates providing far less than expected. Elinor, eldest and sensible, moderates family grief and counsels prudence; Marianne is passionate and imprudent; young Margaret absorbs Marianne’s romance. The household copes with bereavement, propriety, and unsettled fortunes and resilience.

characters.name: the old gentleman (the late owner / uncle)
characters.personality: affectionate toward visiting family, fond of the little child
characters.lifespan: dies; will leaves Norland entailed to his nephew and nephew's son

characters.name: Mr. Henry Dashwood
characters.lifespan: inherits Norland but survives his uncle only a short time (one twelvemonth); dies leaving widow and daughters with limited funds

characters.name: Mrs. Dashwood (Henry's widow)
characters.personality: affectionate, tender, prone to strong feeling and grief
characters.lifespan: widowed and left dependent with her three daughters

characters.name: Mr. John Dashwood
characters.personality: cold-hearted, somewhat selfish but outwardly proper and respected
characters.lifespan: heir to Norland; promises assistance to his stepmother and half-sisters but is influenced to limit his generosity

characters.name: Mrs. John Dashwood (Fanny)
characters.lifespan: installs herself mistress of Norland and persuades her husband to restrict support to his half-sisters

characters.name: Harry (the child, heir)
characters.personality: typical young child: imperfect articulation, self-willed, engaging
characters.lifespan: entitled heir under the entail; his interest is prioritized in family decisions

characters.name: Elinor Dashwood
characters.personality: sensible, cool-headed, judicious, affectionate; the family's counsellor
characters.lifespan: remains to advise and moderate family grief and practical affairs

characters.name: Marianne Dashwood
characters.age: est. late teens (not explicitly given in excerpt)
characters.lifespan: gives way to strong sensibility and grief; her temperament shapes household conduct

characters.name: Margaret Dashwood
characters.personality: good-humored, well-disposed, influenced by Marianne's romance
characters.lifespan: remains with her mother and sisters, adopting some of Marianne's romantic notions

locations.name: Norland Park (fictional estate, placed in Sussex in the novel)
